close_reason: Completed in docs commit 042e01d - updated toolchain-toml.md with version pinning examples
description:
The user manual currently shows toolchain declarations like:

```toml
[toolchains]
go = {}
rust = {}
```

While these are fully reproducible (via the underlying Nix layer), they're not particularly interesting from a specification perspective. The documentation should showcase the versioned toolchain syntax:

```toml
[toolchains]
go.version = "1.25.3"
rust.version = "1.82.0"
python.version = "3.12"
```

This makes the documentation more compelling and demonstrates the value proposition of explicit version control.

**Files to update:**
- docs/user-manual/src/getting-started/project-setup.md
- docs/user-manual/src/configuration/toolchain-toml.md
- docs/user-manual/src/vision/core-principles.md (Level 2 example)
- Any other files showing toolchain.toml examples

**Depends on:** turnkey-xcpo (Implement versioned toolchain registry) - the version syntax needs to actually work before we document it.
title: Update user manual to show versioned toolchain examples
